summary: Gruber reviews Apple's October 2022 product updates, praising the simplified Apple TV 4K lineup while dissecting the increasingly messy iPad product matrix. The new entry-level iPad adopts the modern all-screen design and moves the front-facing camera to the landscape side, but creates friction by only supporting the Lightning-based Apple Pencil 1 despite switching to USB-C. The iPad Pro gets an M2 chip bump and Pencil hover detection but frustratingly keeps its camera on the portrait side and lacks an Esc key on its keyboard. Gruber argues the lineup is in an awkward transitional phase, with incompatible accessories, inconsistent camera placements, and a keyboard accessory schism that defies explanation.
key_points: The Apple TV 4K lineup is now simpler and better-priced, with the USB-C remote serving as a signal that iPhone will follow suit
The new 10th-gen iPad correctly moves the front-facing camera to the landscape side, but the iPad Pro inexplicably still has it on the portrait side despite being the model most used for video calls
Apple Pencil 1's Lightning requirement creates a bizarre dongle situation with the USB-C 10th-gen iPad, though this makes pragmatic sense for schools with existing Pencil 1 fleets
The keyboard accessory fragmentation is the most inexplicable problem — the new iPad, iPad Air, and iPad Pro all use different, incompatible keyboards
Most of the iPad lineup's awkwardness can be understood as a transitional snapshot, but the keyboard incompatibility has no good explanation
money_quotes: And we finally have a dead canary in the 'Is the iPhone switching from Lightning to USB-C next year?' coal mine: the Apple TV Remote now charges via USB-C.
It makes sense if you now think of the 9th-gen iPad as the iPad SE — just without the 'SE' name.
Better a dongle for the Pencil than another generation of iPads on Lightning.
If you really want an iPad Pro with the camera on the long side, sit this cycle out.
'The 10th-gen iPads still work with Pencil 1' is a feature, not a shortcoming, for schools.
The iPad lineup as of this week — including accessories — isn't where anyone would want it to be.
Whatever debate there was over this 10 years ago when the iPad was new, it's settled in today's world of iPad-as-laptop keyboards and ubiquitous teleconferencing for meetings.
key_insight: Apple's iPad lineup is in an awkward transitional phase where most inconsistencies are forgivable as snapshots in time, but the incompatible keyboard accessory ecosystem is a self-inflicted wound with no good justification.
